text:
On June 20, a total of 33 local Special Olympics athletes and volunteers along with police officers and supporters, for a total of 55, participated in the annual Law Enforcement Torch Run for Special Olympics in Elliot Lake.

TheOPP Torch Run started at ScotiaBank and ended at the OPP detachment in Elliot Lake.The Law Enforcement Torch Run is a fundraiser for Special Olympics across the province.

The local Law Enforcement Torch Run raised $7,365.

In addition, Foodland hosted a barbecue fundraiser for Special Olympics on June 15, which raised $2,095, says Julie Nykyforak, of Elliot Lake Special Olympics.

“The Foodland barbeque was definitely a success. We would like to thank Foodland and their suppliers for hosting the barbeque,” says Nykyforak. “The support from this wonderful community was felt.”

They are also grateful to the OPP for their continued support for Special Olympics. The two events combine raised a total of $9,460.

The funds raised are used to cover travel and other expenses for Special Olympics competitions. Throughout the year local athletes participate in: track and field, snowshoeing, swimming and bowling.

description: Torch run and barbeque raise $9K-plus for Special Olympics
